_about: SPEC-004 / DR-009 — hand-assigned expected tiers for SWE-bench-Verified instances. Patches live in gitignored .data/; only these labels are committed.

_rubric_semantic._intent: Tier = how much understanding/design the change demands, judged from the problem statement INDEPENDENT of diff size. A 1-line fix to a subtle algorithmic bug is high tier; a large mechanical change is low. This deliberately decouples labels from the size-based classifier so disagreements expose what size cannot see.
_rubric_semantic.T1: Mechanical / local. Obvious fix once located. Trivial attr, swapped arg, repr.
_rubric_semantic.T2: Bounded feature or fix in one subsystem; standard understanding.
_rubric_semantic.T3: Subtle cross-cutting bug or design — requires understanding non-obvious interactions (ORM internals, tz math, symbolic algebra, async middleware).

_baseline_note: An earlier 24-instance run used SIZE-based labels and scored 95.8% exact (labels correlated with the size-based classifier by construction). These semantic labels are the real test; expect lower exact accuracy where small diffs hide hard bugs.
